name: WorkMesh
tagline: Docs-first, MCP-ready project/task orchestration in plain text next to your code.

steps: say: Bootstrap WorkMesh in this repo. Use MCP if available, otherwise CLI.
say: Use WorkMesh to document this feature end to end: tasks, PRD updates, and decisions.

bootstrap_behavior.modern_workmesh_layout: run health checks, show context, and suggest next work
bootstrap_behavior.legacy_layout: run migrate audit/plan/apply and continue on modern layout
bootstrap_behavior.long_lived_clone_workflow: keep work moving now and recommend migration to canonical repo + worktrees

feature_prompt: Use WorkMesh for this feature. Create/update PRD, break down tasks with acceptance criteria and definition of done, keep context current, and track decisions in Truth Ledger.

task_quality_policy.definition_of_done_rule: Definition of Done must include outcome-based completion criteria, not only hygiene checks.

validation_behavior.done_tasks: missing/incomplete sections or hygiene-only DoD produce errors

cli_fallback.behavior: detect repo state (new/modern/legacy), initialize or migrate as needed, seed missing context, and suggest next tasks

agent_targets: CLI agents (Codex and similar MCP clients)
GUI agents that support command-based MCP

systemd_notes: service install-systemd writes or updates a unit file and runs systemctl daemon-reload
default unit name is workmesh-service.service and can be overridden with --unit-name
use --dry-run --print-unit to preview unit content without writing files
lan_safety: if binding non-localhost, configure an auth token

docker_sample.compose: cd docker/workmesh-service && WORKMESH_REPO_ROOT=/abs/path WORKMESH_AUTH_TOKEN=<token> docker compose up --build -d

config_helpers.set_global: workmesh --root . config set --scope global --key auto_session_default --value true --json

defaults.archive_status_override: pass --status one or more times to archive any specific state, including non-terminal states

auto_session.default: ON in interactive non-CI terminals, OFF in CI/non-interactive contexts

guarantees: Critical tracking writes use lock-safe and atomic storage primitives.
Mutable snapshots are versioned and use CAS update semantics.
Trailing malformed JSONL is tolerated by event readers.
Safe recovery is available through doctor fix-storage pathways.
CLI and MCP expose the same storage recovery contract.

workstreams.summary: Workstreams let you manage multiple parallel streams of work in the same repo (often one git worktree per stream), with durable pointers to context, sessions, and per-stream scope/objective.

behavior: From the canonical checkout (with a real HEAD commit), workstream create auto-provisions a new git worktree by default unless you pass --existing or explicit --path/--branch.
Auto-provision uses worktrees_dir when set; otherwise it defaults to <repo_parent>/<repo_name>.worktrees/.
From a non-canonical git worktree checkout, workstream create binds to the current worktree by default (no new worktree).
If the target worktree path is already bound to a workstream, workstream create returns the existing workstream (already_exists=true) instead of creating a duplicate.

workstreams.auto_linking: When a workstream is active in a worktree, session save and worktree attach/detach keep the stream's session/worktree pointers updated automatically.
workstreams.restore: After reboot (or losing terminals), run `workmesh --root . workstream restore --json` to get per-stream resume commands (path, session id, objective/scope, next task).
workstreams.resume_single: To get resume commands for one stream: `workmesh --root . workstream show <id-or-key> --restore --json`.

adopt_clones.next: Bind a workstream to the created worktree with `workstream create --existing` (the adoption plan includes the exact command).

legacy_note.automatic: bootstrap already handles legacy backlog/focus structures
